frontend.justification: Static SPA required by CON-1; Vite provides fast HMR and ES2020 output for Canvas optimization
frontend.why_not_alternatives: Canvas-based game does not need component framework; TypeScript provides type safety for complex game state; Vite outperforms Webpack for build speed

rendering.justification: Canvas excels at high-frequency updates (60 FPS) required for smooth game play; supports overlay rendering for ghost snake and live snake coexistence per FR-8, FR-9
rendering.why_not_alternatives: WebGL adds unnecessary complexity for 2D grid rendering; SVG and DOM updates too slow for smooth 30×30 grid with frequent cell state changes

state_management.justification: Single-player game has no shared cross-component state tree; in-memory classes provide direct control over state mutations and replay recording per FR-6
state_management.why_not_alternatives: No need for middleware or complex state tree; direct class instances sufficient for game engine architecture

persistence.approach: localStorage for replay storage; in-memory arrays for active game states
persistence.justification: Replay records must persist across sessions per FR-7; localStorage provides browser-native persistence without server dependency (CON-1)
persistence.why_not_alternatives: localStorage sufficient for typical replay size (<100KB per game); IndexedDB adds async complexity; server violates CON-1

input_handling.approach: Keyboard event listeners with input buffering queue
input_handling.justification: Input buffering required to prevent reverse direction in single move per FR-11 (correctness bar); queue ensures rapid key presses do not cause invalid moves
input_handling.why_not_alternatives: Direct mapping does not buffer; polling adds unnecessary RAF overhead

recording_serialization.approach: JSON serialization of game states to array; compact integer encoding for snake segments and food
recording_serialization.justification: Bit-exact replay required per FR-10 (correctness bar); JSON serialization is simple and browser-compatible; integer encoding reduces memory for long games
recording_serialization.why_not_alternatives: Binary format adds complexity; JSON sufficient for target size (100 states × 50 bytes ≈ 5KB per game); delta encoding not necessary for correctness

testing.justification: Vitest integrates natively with Vite; jsdom sufficient for non-rendering logic tests; Playwright tests real canvas rendering and user interactions in actual browser per FR-10 (bit-exactness verification)
testing.why_vitest: Faster than Jest; zero-config with Vite
testing.why_playwright: Only reliable way to test Canvas rendering and replay accuracy across browsers

constraints_respected: CON-1: No server-side runtime (static SPA only)
CON-3: 30×30 grid fits within performance budget at 60 FPS
CON-4: Replay speeds limited to discrete multipliers (0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4×)

risks.description: Canvas is not inherently accessible; requires ARIA labels and keyboard navigation per WCAG guidelines
risks.mitigation: Add aria-label='game grid' to canvas element; implement full keyboard navigation for menus; document in accessibility section of README

risks.description: Long games (1000+ ticks) could consume significant memory; localStorage limit ~5-10MB per domain
risks.mitigation: Implement state pruning (keep only last 500 states); warn user when storage is full; offer export to file

risks.description: If replay uses different random seed than original game, food positions will diverge; violates FR-10 (bit-exact)
risks.mitigation: Serialize RNG seed with each game state; use seeded PRNG (e.g., mulberry32) not Math.random()

risks.description: Multiple tabs running Snake simultaneously could conflict on localStorage writes
risks.mitigation: Use tab ID in localStorage keys; implement timestamp-based conflict resolution

ambiguities: localStorage size limits vary by browser (5-10MB typical); no explicit size target in requirements
Replay speeds (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x) are theoretical; actual FPS depends on browser and host machine performance
Ghost snake visual distinction (color, opacity, dashing) left to implementation; no design spec provided
